title: Mackaya atroviridis
taxonomy: Mackaya atroviridis belongs to the family Acanthaceae, which includes flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or thorny flower. The genus Mackaya is a small group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Mackaya atroviridis is specifically classified as a species of flowering plant that is native to tropical Africa. It is a shrub-like herb with small, purple flowers and is also known as the "forest flower" in some regions. Mackaya atroviridis is a relatively rare species, making it a valuable discovery for botanists and conservationists.
